methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RMFG is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Kenneth Cassel that has raised $5M+ to date. The comparable universe consists of 9 peers across robotics and manufacturing—1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Of the standard valuation triangulation methods, only stage-progression analysis is applicable here. This method uses the company's $5M total raised as a baseline value proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding an implied valuation of $12M. Peer-median analysis cannot be reliably applied; while 11 robotics and manufacturing peers exist in the broader dataset, none qualify as narrow comparables on stage, timing, or valuation tier—the threshold of three datapoints necessary for median-based benchmarking is not met.

The triangulated valuation range is $11M to $14M, with a median point of $12M. This range is derived from a single method and therefore carries material uncertainty. Key unknowns include the maturity of RMFG's technology, unit economics, and go-to-market traction—variables that would sharpen the analysis if available. The absence of same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the robotics sector limits the precision of any multiple-based anchor. Investors should treat this range as directional rather than definitive and conduct primary diligence to validate stage-appropriate milestones.
